quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 12 Mixed Review
quiz.subtitle: Unit 12 Quiz 11: Trigonometric Identities & Equations — Heavy Hitters (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Unit 12: Trigonometric Identities & Equations — High-End Mixed Review

questions.question: Simplify $\frac{\cos x}{1 - \sin x}$ to an expression involving secant and tangent.
questions.explanation: Multiply by conjugate $1+\sin x$: $\frac{\cos x(1+\sin x)}{1-\sin^2 x} = \frac{\cos x(1+\sin x)}{\cos^2 x} = \frac{1+\sin x}{\cos x} = \sec x + \tan x$.

questions.question: Find the exact value of $\sin(75^{\circ})$ using the sum formula.
Find the exact value of $\cos\left(\frac{\pi}{12}\right)$ using the difference formula.
Given $\sin\theta = \frac{3}{5}$ with $\theta$ in Quadrant I, find $\cos(2\theta)$.
Solve $\cos^2 x - \sin^2 x = \frac{1}{2}$ for $x$ in $[0, 2\pi)$ using a double-angle identity.
The height of a Ferris wheel rider is $h(t) = 50 - 40\cos(\frac{\pi}{15}t)$ in feet, $t$ in seconds. Find the first time the rider is at height $70$ feet.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered Unit 12: simplifying with Pythagorean identities, proving identities, sum and difference formulas, double-angle formulas, solving basic, multiple-angle, and quadratic trig equations, and applying to real-world problems.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key areas to review: (1) $\sin^2\theta = 1 - \cos^2\theta = (1-\cos\theta)(1+\cos\theta)$, (2) Sum/Difference: $\sin(A\pm B)$ and $\cos(A\pm B)$ formulas, (3) For multiple-angle equations, find all solutions for the argument in the expanded interval, then divide, (4) Quadratic in trig form: substitute $u = \sin x$ or $u = \cos x$, factor, solve.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort on challenging material. Focus on: (1) Recognizing when to apply double-angle identities like $\cos^2 x - \sin^2 x = \cos(2x)$, (2) For equations, always consider all quadrants where the trig value appears, (3) When solving $\cos(2x) = a$, solve for $2x$ in $[0, 4\pi)$ to get all solutions for $x$ in $[0, 2\pi)$.
scoreMessages.0: Review Unit 12 fundamentals: (1) Pythagorean IDs: $\sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = 1$, $\tan^2\theta + 1 = \sec^2\theta$, $1 + \cot^2\theta = \csc^2\theta$, (2) Sum/Difference: $\sin(A\pm B) = \sin A\cos B \pm \cos A\sin B$, $\cos(A\pm B) = \cos A\cos B \mp \sin A\sin B$, (3) Double-angle: $\sin(2\theta) = 2\sin\theta\cos\theta$, $\cos(2\theta) = \cos^2\theta - \sin^2\theta$, (4) Solve trig equations by factoring, using identities, and considering all quadrants.
